_description: Python3 healthcheck command vocabulary. Each token value is a python3 -c one-liner executed via shell=True by GrammarRunner. Demonstrates that grammar playbooks can run Python code, not just shell utilities.
py_check_kernel: python3 -c 'import platform, sys; print("--- kernel / runtime ---"); print("Kernel :", platform.release()); print("OS     :", platform.system(), platform.machine()); print("Python :", sys.version)'
py_check_uptime: python3 -c 'import os; data=open("/proc/uptime").read().split(); u=float(data[0]); h,r=divmod(int(u),3600); m,s=divmod(r,60); ld=os.getloadavg(); print("--- uptime / load ---"); print(f"Uptime : {h}h {m}m {s}s"); print(f"Load   : {ld[0]:.2f}  {ld[1]:.2f}  {ld[2]:.2f}  (1/5/15 min)")'
py_check_cpu: python3 -c 'import os; lines=[l for l in open("/proc/cpuinfo") if "model name" in l or "cpu MHz" in l]; print("--- cpu ---"); print("CPUs  :", os.cpu_count()); [print(l.rstrip()) for l in lines[:3]]'
py_check_disk: python3 -c 'import shutil; print("--- disk usage ---"); [print(f"{mp:<8} {shutil.disk_usage(mp).total//1073741824:5d}GB total  {shutil.disk_usage(mp).used//1073741824:5d}GB used  {shutil.disk_usage(mp).free//1073741824:5d}GB free  ({shutil.disk_usage(mp).used/shutil.disk_usage(mp).total*100:.1f}%)") for mp in ["/", "/tmp"]]'
py_check_processes: python3 -c 'import subprocess; r=subprocess.run(["ps","aux","--sort=-%mem","--no-headers"],capture_output=True,text=True); print("--- top 8 processes by memory (via python subprocess) ---"); print("".join(r.stdout.splitlines(keepends=True)[:8]))'
py_check_services: python3 -c 'import subprocess; r=subprocess.run(["systemctl","list-units","--state=failed","--no-pager"],capture_output=True,text=True); print("--- failed systemd services ---"); print(r.stdout.strip() or "None")'
py_check_ports: python3 -c 'import subprocess; r=subprocess.run(["ss","-tuln"],capture_output=True,text=True); print("--- open ports (via python subprocess + ss) ---"); print(r.stdout[:600])'
